title: calamagrostis inexpansa subsp. micrantha
intro: Calamagrostis inexpansa subsp. micrantha, also known as a type of grass, is a plant species that belongs to the Poaceae family and is native to mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Calamagrostis inexpansa subsp. micrantha include its ability to reduce inflammation, ease pain, and promote healing, as well as its potential in treating digestive issues and fever. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can be used topically to apply its medicinal properties directly to the affected area.
